skill.name: hubspot-integration
skill.description: Expert patterns for HubSpot CRM integration including OAuth authentication, CRM objects, associations, batch operations, webhooks, and custom objects. Covers Node.js and Python SDKs.
skill.summary: Expert patterns for HubSpot CRM integration including OAuth authentication, CRM objects, associations, batch operations, webhooks, and custom objects.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 2 false positives for 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3 and 46. These flags were triggered by documentation text mentioning 'authentication' and are not actual security issues. The file contains only legitimate HubSpot CRM integration patterns documentation with no malicious code, prompt injection attempts, or security vulnerabilities.

low_findings.title: False Positive - Cryptographic Reference
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'authentication' keyword as weak cryptographic algorithm. This is documentation text only, not executable code.

content.user_title: Integrate HubSpot CRM with Claude Code
content.value_statement: Build HubSpot CRM integrations faster using expert patterns for authentication, CRUD operations, and webhooks. Eliminate API complexity with proven implementation patterns for Node.js and Python SDKs.
actual_capabilities: Generate HubSpot OAuth 2.0 authentication code for public apps
Create private app token authentication patterns for single-account integrations
Implement CRUD operations for HubSpot CRM objects (contacts, companies, deals)
Build batch operations to handle multiple records efficiently
Configure webhook handlers for real-time HubSpot event processing
Design custom object schemas and integration patterns
limitations: Requires valid HubSpot API credentials or OAuth tokens
Does not provide actual HubSpot account or API access
SDK code generation requires understanding of Node.js or Python
Rate limits and API quotas must be managed in implementation

use_cases.title: Contact Synchronization
use_cases.description: Automatically sync customer data between your application and HubSpot CRM contacts

use_cases.title: Webhook Event Processing
use_cases.description: Build handlers to process HubSpot lifecycle events like contact creation or deal stage changes

use_cases.title: Custom Object Integration
use_cases.description: Create and manage custom HubSpot objects for specialized business data beyond standard CRM records
use_cases.target_user: SaaS teams extending HubSpot for unique use cases

prompt_templates.title: Basic Contact Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a HubSpot contact using Node.js SDK with email, first name, and last name properties

prompt_templates.title: OAuth Authentication Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement HubSpot OAuth 2.0 flow for a public app with token refresh logic

prompt_templates.title: Batch Import Operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Write code to batch import 1000 contacts into HubSpot using batch API endpoints

prompt_templates.title: Webhook Handler Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Express.js webhook handler for HubSpot contact creation events with signature verification

output_examples.input: Create a HubSpot contact in Python with private app token
output_examples.output: Generates Python code using hubspot-api-client with ContactObject creation, error handling for 4xx responses, and environment variable configuration for HUBSPOT_API_KEY

output_examples.input: Set up OAuth authentication for Node.js
output_examples.output: Produces Node.js code implementing OAuth authorization flow, access token storage, refresh token logic, and middleware for protected API calls

output_examples.input: Create webhook handler for deal updates
output_examples.output: Returns Express route handler with HubSpot signature verification, event parsing logic, and async processing pipeline for deal property changes

best_practices: Always use batch API endpoints instead of individual requests when creating or updating multiple records
Implement webhook signature verification to ensure requests originate from HubSpot
Store OAuth refresh tokens securely and implement automatic token refresh before expiry
anti_patterns: Avoid using deprecated API keys for authentication
Do not poll HubSpot APIs for data changes when webhooks are available
Never hardcode API tokens or OAuth credentials in source code

faq.question: What HubSpot authentication methods are supported?
faq.answer: This skill covers OAuth 2.0 for public apps and private app tokens for single-account integrations. OAuth is recommended for production applications with multiple users.

faq.question: Which programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill provides patterns and code examples for both Node.js and Python SDKs, covering the most common backend integration scenarios.

faq.question: Can this skill create HubSpot accounts or API keys?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates code patterns for integration. You must create your own HubSpot developer account, app, and API credentials through the HubSpot developer portal.

faq.question: How do I handle HubSpot API rate limits?
faq.answer: The skill provides patterns for implementing exponential backoff and batch operations to optimize within rate limits. Your implementation should track usage and implement queuing for high-volume operations.

faq.question: Are webhooks better than polling for HubSpot data?
faq.answer: Yes, webhooks are significantly more efficient for real-time updates. This skill includes patterns for webhook handler implementation, signature verification, and event processing.

faq.question: Can I create custom HubSpot objects with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill covers custom object schema design and the API patterns needed to create, read, update, and delete custom object records through the HubSpot API.
